action_choice_hints: Action moves the robot's base position closer to the target's center in Euclidean distance.
Action direction is aligned with the vector from the robot to the target.
Action maintains a smooth, consistent approach trajectory toward the target without abrupt changes in direction.
Action avoids overshooting or bypassing the target, ensuring progress is monotonic toward the goal.
Action maintains safe clearance from the target until contact is intended (no premature intersection).
Action includes rotational adjustments that align the robot's orientation with the approach direction to the target.
Action does not activate manipulation or vacuum controls unless at the target (these remain inactive during approach).
feature_family_hints: Relative-position features: Needed to compute the vector and distance from the robot to the target for goal-directed movement.
Directional-alignment features: Needed to assess whether the action's direction matches the direction toward the target.
Progress-to-goal features: Needed to measure reduction in distance to the target and ensure monotonic progress.
Collision/intersection features: Needed to detect when the robot is about to or has contacted the target.
Orientation-alignment features: Needed to align the robot's heading with the approach vector to the target.
Action smoothness/consistency features: Needed to ensure actions do not introduce abrupt or erratic changes in trajectory.
causal_transition_hints: Actions that move the robot closer to the target tend to cause a reduction in the robot-target distance.
Actions aligned with the robot-to-target vector tend to produce monotonic progress toward the target.
Actions that maintain safe clearance avoid premature intersection with the target until the final approach.
Actions that include appropriate rotational adjustments tend to align the robot's orientation with the approach direction, facilitating efficient contact.
Actions that do not activate manipulation or vacuum controls avoid unintended state changes in the robot's end-effector or gripper during approach.
If the action deviates from the approach vector, progress toward the target slows or becomes non-monotonic.
